title: cineraria palustris
intro: Cineraria palustris, also known as marsh cineraria or marsh dandelion, is a flowering plant that belongs to the dandelion family. Traditionally, the plant's leaves, roots, and stems have been used in herbal medicine due to their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, diuretic, and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as relieving digestive issues, reducing fever, and supporting liver and kidney function. Cineraria palustris can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and potential interactions with other medications.
